The loaded diff shows matching-oriented source reshaping, including local lifetime/scope changes, helper inlines, casts, PAD_STACK, volatile local use, and data/string clustering in mncount and tydisplay.

title: ftPr_SpecialN.c work

The author explicitly noted that some changes were not 100% matches but were close enough to push.

title: Small fixes to THPDec
author: PsiLupan
summary: PR 2304 made focused THP decoder changes in the Dolphin THP header and implementation: recovered/reshaped several THP struct fields, changed THPInit to void and added low-level cache/locked-cache setup calls, passed THPFileInfo explicitly into the 512x448 iMCU row decoder path, and made small formatting/control-flow edits. The bot report showed net matched-code improvement and one new matched function, but also one broken match and one unmatched-item regression; there were no human review comments and the PR was closed unmerged.

title: Implement nearly all remaining "proxy" functions
author: lukechampine
summary: Large decomp-matching pass that replaced many `/// #` placeholders for simple proxy/trampoline functions with C bodies that only call one other function. The work was found with a new `table-typer dups` command, which grouped duplicated asm bodies and identified unmatched functions sharing the same opcode sequence as known proxy functions. The decomp.dev bot reported 114 new matches and +3648 matched bytes. title: Restore `ftAction_80071028`
author: ribbanya
summary: Restored the decompiled implementation of fighter script handler `ftAction_80071028`, replacing a fully commented-out draft with matching C. summary: Matched several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c, centered on demo/game-mode enter and rendering callbacks. The PR added typed static state for lbl_804736B0 instead of raw array indexing, implemented player/demo setup through ftDemo and Player APIs, added JObj scene setup and animation callbacks, and tightened related function prototypes in gm_1832.h. A few neighboring signatures were widened from s8/u8 to int in gm_1BA8, player, and vi0102 to support matching call ABI/codegen. No substantive review feedback was present in the provided slice.

The PR implemented Yoshi SpecialN helper fn_8012E44C, event Yoshi egg spawn logic, and two thrown Yoshi egg item helpers, while tightening nearby header prototypes. title: Partially match `lb_00F9`

The work was supported by more precise `DynamicsData`/`PolymorphicDesc` and `ColorOverlay` typing, corrected prototypes for image/DVD/GX-related helpers, and small downstream callsite updates in fighter dynamics and item collision code.

title: allow for specific ifdef preservation in ctx
author: thefoxcam
summary: Reworked tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py so the pcpp path uses a custom Preprocessor subclass instead of invoking pcpp with a static command-line flag list. The custom preprocessor preserves selected conditional blocks keyed on M2C while still defining M2CTX for context generation, carrying over the original include paths, defines, undefines, compression/comment behavior, and MWERKS compatibility assumptions. Header guards and inline/type helper conditionals across effect, fighter, ground, item, menu, and toy headers were switched from #ifdef M2CTX to #ifdef M2C so those decomp.me/m2c-specific alternatives can survive into ctx output rather than being eagerly resolved during context preprocessing.

title: Create separate inner non-fighter HurtCapsule type
author: r-burns
summary: Split fighter hurt capsule storage from the generic lb/it HurtCapsule layout by introducing/using FighterHurtCapsule for Fighter::hurt_capsules and passing its inner .capsule member to generic collision-library routines. The stated motivation was to fix struct size discrepancies between fighter code and lb/item code. The PR also cleaned up item-field accesses that had been abusing xACC_itemHurtbox[1] members as unrelated Vec2/float storage, replacing them with direct xB54/xB5C/xB60/xB64 fields, and adjusted affected prototypes and symbol sizes.

title: Split out game mode files from gm_1B03
author: r-burns
summary: Split the large game-mode translation unit gm_1B03.c into separate mode-focused files for Classic, Adventure, All-Star, and Multi-Man, with matching split ranges and build configuration updates. The PR mostly reorganized existing nonmatching and matched fragments, pruned gm_1B03.h prototypes into new headers, and made a few type/callback cleanups that produced a net decomp.dev report of 10 new matches, 9 corresponding broken matches from the old unit, and +160 matched bytes.

title: More table typing
author: lukechampine
summary: Typed more function-pointer table entries across game-mode and stage code, replacing many `UNK_RET ... (UNK_PARAMS)` declarations with concrete signatures such as `void (*)(MinorScene*)`, `void (*)(HSD_GObj*)`, `void (*)(int)`, and StageData callback shapes. The PR also declared and typed the `gm_803DF94C` table via a new `gm_803DF94C_t` struct, adjusted `gm_803DE1B8_MinorScenes` symbol data to 4-byte entries, and expanded the Go `tools/table-typer` utility to understand `MinorScene`, `StageData`, and `gm_803DF94C_t` tables. The author noted this reduced remaining asm tables containing `UNK_RET` functions to 69. decomp.dev reported no GALE01 output changes.

title: Table typer: StageCallbacks
author: lukechampine
summary: Generalized and improved tools/table-typer, then applied it to StageCallbacks and related table-derived callback signatures. The main decomp-facing result was changing StageCallbacks and many stage callback functions from generic HSD_GObj* parameters to Ground_GObj*, with necessary forward includes in affected headers. The tool also gained additional table type definitions, a fixtab subcommand, an unk subcommand for finding UNK_RET functions referenced by asm tables, better asm object parsing, and documentation. A decomp.dev bot report showed no GALE01 binary changes.

title: Just about match ftCo_DamageIce and improve used names

title: iftime matches
author: r-burns

The changes are narrowly focused on decomp matching: introducing a local pointer alias to ifTime_data, preserving a call boundary through a dontinline-style wrapper, forcing a byte temporary for HSD_GObj_804D7849, adding PAD_STACK(8), and adjusting local variable ordering/types to reproduce target assembly.

title: match most of iftime
author: BR-
summary: Matched and named most of the interface timer unit, adding a new C implementation for src/melee/if/iftime.c and replacing address-style ifTime/fn symbols with semantic names for timer creation, update, visibility, countdown, reset, and teardown. The PR also propagated those names into headers and game-mode call sites, adjusted GALE01 split/symbol sizes for iftime data/BSS, and renamed a StartMeleeRules bitfield to timer_shows_hours to support the timer formatting logic. The decomp.dev bot reported 8 new fully matched iftime functions and partial matches for SetTime, CreateTimers, and UpdateTimers.

title: Include main header first
author: r-burns
summary: Updated the repository .clang-format include-category priorities so clang-format places a C file's corresponding main header first, e.g. fighter.c including fighter.h before all other includes. The rationale was to make each main header self-sufficient: if the corresponding .h is included first in its .c file, missing standalone dependencies are exposed instead of being hidden by earlier transitive includes.

summary: Matched several character select menu functions in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c and advanced three larger unmatched functions by tightening local/static data layout, prototypes, and HSD animation/fog/audio calls. The decomp.dev bot reported +944 matched bytes: six new 100% matches and improvements to mnCharSel_8025D5AC, mnCharSel_8025EE8C, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave. Supporting changes split CSS data structs, moved BSS/static declarations into mncharsel.static.h, refined external function prototypes, and corrected GALE01 data symbol boundaries for mnCharSel data and SJIS strings.

title: Use major scene enum values
author: r-burns
summary: Replaced raw major-scene numeric IDs throughout game-mode code and scene data tables with existing MJ_* enum names, including sentinel use of MJ_COUNT. The PR also clarified a couple of function parameter names related to pending/current major scenes. The decomp bot reported no GALE01 binary changes, so this was a semantics-preserving readability/header cleanup rather than a behavior change.

title: add enum for minor scenes/class id
author: sadkellz
summary: Added a MinorSceneKind enum to src/melee/gm/forward.h, enumerating minor scene/class IDs from MN_TITLE through MN_CAMERA_VS with MN_COUNT as the sentinel. The change is header-only and provides named constants plus offset comments and contextual notes for menus, cutscenes, movies, tournament screens, memory card prompts, staff roll, and other game-mode minor scenes. decomp.dev reported no GALE01 changes, so this was a semantic/naming improvement without binary impact.

title: Improve some ftCo_ItemThrow matches
author: r-burns
summary: Improved matching and readability for several common fighter item-throw routines by replacing many M2C_FIELD/raw-offset accesses with typed Fighter/ftCo_Fighter fields, GET_FIGHTER, named callbacks, input fields, item state fields, and motion-variable fields. The PR also corrected ftCommonData layout around offset 0x3FC, allowing code to use p_ftCommonData->x3FC directly, and removed an obsolete global label symbol at ftCo_800959A0 after the function boundary/match improved.

title: fix outstanding cror comparisons
author: lukechampine
summary: Replaced remaining m2c-emitted CROR comparison placeholders with explicit inclusive relational comparisons, mostly in fighter item-throw logic and sound-test decrement clamps. Also removed stale commented CROR notes where the surrounding code already used the correct comparison. The PR is a small decomp-matching cleanup that converts ambiguous equality checks plus M2C_ERROR markers into readable >= or <= conditions.

title: Work around `ninja diff` return code
author: ribbanya
summary: CI/tooling PR that worked around unreliable `ninja diff` status handling in the link job. The workflow now preserves the `ninja` build result, streams `ninja diff` output line by line, marks failure when output contains `ERROR`, and exits with the accumulated result. It also corrected GitHub Actions warning annotation syntax in `tools/diff_changes.py`. A large `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` update changed many symbol scopes and local autogenerated labels, but the decomp.dev bot reported `No changes`, so the substantive intent appears to be CI/reporting rather than game-code matching.

title: gmtou matches
author: sadkellz
summary: Matched several Tournament mode gmtou functions and tightened related game-mode declarations. The PR replaced multiple gmtou stubs with C implementations for on-enter/on-leave archive setup and cleanup, animation timer callbacks, and JObj animation-frame selection logic. It also introduced Tournament-specific data structures such as TmData, TmBoxArrays, and TmAnimTimers, converted gm_804771C4 from an untyped array placeholder to TmData, and improved prototypes in gmtou.h and gm_17C0.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS/UNK_T to concrete HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, void, char*, and TmData* types. Review feedback highlighted an important header hygiene issue: .static.h headers containing static/data definitions should only be included from .c files, not public headers, because including them elsewhere can perturb data/bss sections.

title: Match lbArchive_80017340, cleanup HSD archive
author: PsiLupan
summary: Matched `lbArchive_80017340` while cleaning up HSD archive parsing/relocation code. The PR changed the relocate routine to take a raw `u8* src` archive buffer instead of an `HSD_ArchiveHeader*`, copied the header from that buffer, computed section pointers using `sizeof(HSD_ArchiveHeader)` and typed archive-info struct sizes, and split relocation application into an inline `Locate` helper. It also replaced magic archive layout constants in `HSD_ArchiveParse` with `sizeof(...)` expressions and adjusted headers/prototypes accordingly.

title: Add translation unit support for decomp.py
author: thefoxcam
summary: Updated tools/decomp.py so its single positional m2c input can be either a function name or a translation-unit path such as melee/it/items/itheiho. The script now attempts to resolve the input as a function via find_obj/has_function; if no object containing that function is found, it treats the input as a translation unit, builds the corresponding ASM path, and invokes m2c without --function. --write remains function-only and now errors for translation-unit input.

summary: Matched and typed several gmmain_lib functions around game-mode state accessors, render-mode selection, reset/startup helpers, and time delta tracking. The PR replaced multiple placeholder stubs and commented-out implementations with real C, refined struct fields in gm/types.h, corrected function prototypes across gm_1601.h, gmmain_lib.h, and toy.h, and adjusted include boundaries so users of gmmain_lib can forward-declare gm types while implementation/static headers include full type definitions.

title: gmmainlib matches
author: r-burns
summary: Matched several gmmain_lib routines and added concrete data definitions for default GameRules and a custom GXRenderModeObj. The PR replaced multiple nonmatching placeholders with C implementations for rules randomization, zeroing substructures, VI render-mode selection, main-lib initialization, and reset-code bookkeeping. It also tightened gmmain_lib.h prototypes and moved pad_F0 into struct gmm_retval_EDBC in types.h, indicating the struct boundary was adjusted to support matching and correct field layout.

title: lbdvd matches
author: r-burns
summary: Matched several lbdvd preload/cache management routines and tightened related lb heap/file prototypes and preload structs. The PR replaced multiple placeholder declarations in src/melee/lb/lbdvd.c with C implementations for heap purge/wait helpers, preload entry allocation, path-to-entry preload registration, persistent heap scene switching, and small spin-wait wrappers. It also adjusted PreloadEntry field signedness, added lbHeap_800158E8/lbHeap_80015900 prototypes, changed lbDvd_803BA68C from a static zero initializer to an extern reset template, and included lb_0192.h for lb_800195D0 wait/yield calls.

title: Match some of gricemt.c and grpura.c

Build configuration, splits, symbols, and headers were updated so the extracted ranges compile as separate NonMatching objects while preserving address layout.

title: Port over THP from Pikmin 2
author: r-burns
summary: Added a large THP decoder implementation by porting THP code/structure knowledge from Pikmin 2 into Melee's Dolphin external SDK area. The PR introduced extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c and extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/thp/thp.h, then renamed several previously anonymous THPDec_* symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to confident THP names. The PR body explains the central matching insight: other decompiled games use a later THP version with a global THPFileInfo pointer instead of passing THPFileInfo into each private function, and adapting to that global-pointer style was enough to match or near-match several functions.

title: Mass rename scene callback functions from scene tables

title: Match more of robj

The PR also tightened RObj type-mask usage, corrected descriptor/reference handling, adjusted function-pointer signatures for expression callbacks, typed the global argument buffer as float*, split several data symbols into strings in symbols.txt, and left only resolveCnsOrientation as the stated remaining unresolved function.

title: Match OS, OSReset
author: r-burns
summary: Matched Dolphin OS.c and OSReset.c by completing missing SDK initialization/reset behavior, adding required internal prototypes and an OSThread API declaration, and flipping both objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The OSInit changes add startup timestamp capture, BI2/debug flag handling, alarm and memory-protection init calls, HID2 masking, arena clearing, and more accurate MetroTRK/debug handling. The OSReset changes add hot reset support, active-thread cancellation, reset-type-specific reboot/reset behavior, cache/LC and memory-clear handling, pad recalibration preservation, and an adjusted OSGetResetCode implementation.

title: Relax `melee-issues` error handling
author: ribbanya
summary: Relaxed error handling in the Rust `melee-issues` tooling so missing or empty Clang diagnostic strings no longer abort processing. title: m2ctx: Pass `--quiet` to error output
author: thefoxcam
summary: Updated the m2ctx tooling so that preprocessing failures respect the `--quiet` flag: the failing command is still printed to stderr, but the full preprocessor output/context dump is suppressed when quiet mode is enabled. The patch threaded a typed `quiet: bool` parameter through `try_import`, `pcpp_import`, and `mwcc_import`, and adjusted error printing to avoid messy trailing-newline concatenation.

title: Rewrite diff workflow to make it compare properly
author: thefoxcam
summary: Reworked the GitHub Actions diff-check workflow so objdiff compares the intended expected and actual reports, then moved the broken-function detection logic out of inline jq and into a new Python helper, tools/diff_changes.py. The workflow now records the original HEAD before checking out the comparison target, rebuilds the expected report there, checks back out HEAD, generates an objdiff changes report, and runs the Python script to fail CI when any previously 100% fuzzy-matched function regresses.

title: Dolphin SDK Matches
author: PsiLupan
summary: Matched several Dolphin SDK AR/AX objects using work from the dolsdk2001 repo. The PR removed assembly for dolphin/ar/ar.s, AXAlloc.s, AXAux.s, and AXProf.s, added C implementations and headers for AR/AX allocation, aux processing, profiling, and voice/parameter-block types, then switched the build configuration to compile the matched C objects. It also corrected symbol sizes for AX stack/aux globals and added OSPhysicalToUncached for ARAM size probing.

title: Fix issues with objalloc
author: PsiLupan
summary: Reworked the baselib objalloc statistics helpers so the inline accessors operate directly on HSD_ObjAllocData instead of an HSD_ObjAllocInfo wrapper, and updated HSD_ObjDumpStat to keep its local reporting table as function-pointer plus label entries. This removed the public HSD_ObjAllocInfo typedef from objalloc.h and changed call sites to explicitly call each GetAllocData function before reading using/freed/peak counters. A small VS Code file association update added xstring as C.

title: Match some ftparts functions
author: r-burns
summary: Matched several previously stubbed ftparts functions and adjusted JObj header helpers needed by those matches. The PR filled in fighter part/JObj/PObj behavior including class changing, custom interpreted JObj loading, PObj matrix setup dispatch with z-scale handling, bone JObj removal/reset, rotation setting, and rotation X/Y getters with quaternion fallback/assert behavior. Header updates included correcting ftParts_800755E8's prototype and adding/strengthening HSD_JObj rotation helper inlines.

title: Misc match improvements and cleanups
author: r-burns
summary: Small cross-cutting cleanup PR motivated by removing M2C_FIELD usage while improving matches in fighter action/collision/material code, common fighter states, sword item code, and lb refract headers. The changes replaced raw offset and bit-mask access with named struct fields, bitfields, GET_FIGHTER/GET_ITEM helpers, typed command-script access, and clearer loop/control-flow shapes where those helped matching.

title: ithassam
author: wyatt-avilla
summary: Added the decompiled Scizor/Hassam item implementation in src/melee/it/items/ithassam.c, including its state table, behavior callbacks, targeting/movement logic, camera-directed behavior, and item variable storage. The PR also promoted needed declarations in camera.h and it_2725.h, added HassamVars to common item types, wired it into Item::xDD4_itemVar, and changed many ithassam-local functions/constants in symbols.txt from global to local. Author notes say it_802CE400 was the only non-matching part and that inlines in it_802CE0C4 and it_802CE400 were omitted due to stack usage.

title: `itfflower`
author: wyatt-avilla
summary: Decompiled the Fire Flower item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itfflower.s with a matching C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itfflower.c, wiring it into the build as Matching, and adding the needed headers, item-var struct, prototypes, and symbol-scope updates. The PR also tightened related declarations such as it_8026DF34 returning bool and ithammer's analogous transition helper returning bool.

title: `itmarumine`
author: wyatt-avilla
summary: Decompiled the Marumine/Electrode item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itmarumine.s with src/melee/it/items/itmarumine.c, wiring the C object into the build, and updating symbols/header declarations. The new C recreates the item state table it_803F7E78, item callbacks from it_802D09D0 through it_802D1410, hitlag effect pause/resume setup, animation state changes, fall/ground transitions, and repeated efSync_Spawn accessory effects. Most internal callbacks and constants were marked local/static in symbols.txt; the PR author specifically noted adding scope:local for fn_802D0F98 because that symbol previously lacked an explicit scope.

title: `itcoin`
author: wyatt-avilla
summary: Added the coin item implementation in src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c with its state table, behavior callbacks, spawn helper it_802F2094, and supporting item variable/attribute structs and prototypes. The PR also corrected related signatures, notably making it_802F2094 and Ground_801C58E0 return Item_GObj* instead of s32, and marked many itcoin-local functions and sdata2 constants local in symbols.txt. Review focused on header hygiene and type conventions: prefer int or bool over s32 where appropriate, prefer UNK_T over M2C_UNK, and ensure new headers have include guards because missing guards break m2ctx.

title: Fix `STATIC_ASSERT` condition
author: ribbanya
summary: Changed the preprocessor condition guarding the custom `STATIC_ASSERT` macro in `src/Runtime/platform.h` from `#ifdef _Static_assert` to `#ifdef __MWERKS__`. This makes the fallback struct-based assertion path explicitly depend on the Metrowerks compiler rather than testing for `_Static_assert`, avoiding the wrong branch selection when `_Static_assert` has been aliased or defined in the surrounding compatibility code.

title: Fix include paths in `sysdolphin`
author: ribbanya
summary: Mass include-path cleanup across src/sysdolphin/baselib to work around an MWCC `-cwd source` header-resolution bug. The PR replaced many angle-bracket baselib includes such as `#include <baselib/jobj.h>` with quoted includes that are either local to the same baselib directory, such as `#include "jobj.h"`, or explicitly rooted as `#include "baselib/jobj.h"` inside headers. This mattered because MWCC resolves quoted includes from the current C file location rather than from the including header's directory when `-cwd source` is used, so insufficiently specific header paths could make unrelated translation units search the wrong directory, e.g. `fighter.c` including `gobj.h` then trying to resolve `melee/ft/jobj.h` instead of the baselib header.

title: frank.py: Restrict rescheduling to .text section
author: r-burns
summary: Updated tools/frank.py so its profiler-based instruction rescheduling operates only on the MWCC .text/code section rather than scanning and rewriting the entire object file. The script now extracts header, .text, and footer using the object code-size marker and a fixed MWCC code offset, runs rescheduling only on sliced code bytes, then reassembles the object. The PR also cleaned up repeated aligned-search logic and tightened safety checks around code-size mismatches and epilogue-local reorder handling.

summary: Split a large item assembly file into smaller item-collision-oriented assembly units. Most of the removed content from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s was reintroduced as new asm/melee/it/itcoll.s and asm/melee/it/it_2725.s files, then both new objects were added to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk immediately after it_266F.s.o. The PR had no body or review discussion, so the motivation is inferred from the title and diff: make the item collision region independently addressable for future decompilation while preserving link order.

title: Remove itHit struct
author: r-burns
summary: Removed the item-local itHit struct and replaced item hitbox uses with the shared HitCapsule layout. The PR rationale was that item collision code passed &Item->x5D4 into functions expecting HitCapsule, and known regions of itHit and HitCapsule were identical. Item::x5D4_hitboxes became an array of small wrappers containing HitCapsule hit plus the trailing x138 field, function prototypes were updated from itHit* to HitCapsule*, and field accesses were translated from copied itHit names to HitCapsule names such as hit.state and hit.damage.

title: Match itparasol
author: r-burns
summary: Matched the Parasol item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itparasol.s with src/melee/it/items/itparasol.c and wiring the C object into obj_files.mk. The new C file reconstructs the item state table, state-transition callbacks, physics/deceleration behavior, collision callbacks, animation-speed wrappers, and a JObj rotation update path. One difficult function, it_8028B1A4, keeps a MUST_MATCH inline-assembly version while also providing a readable C version outside MUST_MATCH. The PR also added itparasol.h prototypes, exposed itColl_BounceOffVictim in it_266F.h, and added HSD_JObjGetRotationY to jobj.h to support the decompiled code.

title: Use GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ throughout item callbacks
author: r-burns
systems: item;item callbacks;capsule item;Fox Illusion item;Fox Laser item;Sheik vanish item

It also replaced a raw Sheik vanish item kind constant with the named It_Kind_Seak_Vanish enum value.

title: Clean up item article data types and accesses
author: r-burns
summary: Replaced several ad-hoc item article wrapper structs and local extern declarations with typed Article** globals declared in it_266F.h, then simplified common, character, Pokemon, and stage item article table accesses to direct array indexing. The cleanup also typed StageInfo.x6B4 as an array of script pointers and introduced ItLGunVars for the light gun special attributes access. This mattered because it removed misleading placeholder structs and made item article table indexing clearer and more reusable across item and ground code.

title: Impove jobj inlines
author: r-burns
summary: Adjusted JObj inline definitions and matrix-access naming after discovering that HSD_JObjSetupMatrix failed to inline into lbcoll and that the old HSD_JObjGetMtx helper was really a matrix-pointer getter. The PR replaced an asm MUST_MATCH copy in lbcollision with a C wrapper matching the inlined setup-matrix behavior, renamed HSD_JObjGetMtx to HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr with a MtxPtr return, and used that helper to simplify/improve matches in fighter and Purin code.

title: Remove redundant declarations

It preserved the better-positioned or better-documented declaration in several cases, added missing forward-header inclusion for scene declarations, and left a note that more redundant declarations remained, especially in melee/ft/ft_08A4.h.

title: Hardcode image name
author: r-burns
summary: CI workflow fix that stops forked repositories from deriving Docker package image names from the fork owner/name. The workflows now set a global IMAGE_REPO to doldecomp/melee and construct images as $IMAGE_REPO/$IMAGE_SUFFIX, removing per-event push/pull_request repository selection and lowercasing shell logic. dump-asm and publish-pages also changed their deploy/push guards from explicit master/main refs to the repository default branch.

summary: Large header/prototype cleanup pass to support building with Metrowerks `-requireprotos`. The PR added many missing function prototypes and forward declarations across MSL, Runtime, Dolphin SDK subsystems, sysdolphin/baselib, fighter, stage, item, and game-mode code; moved callback typedefs into forward headers where needed; and adjusted several C function definitions to match explicit prototypes, especially low-level Dolphin functions such as PPCArch and CARD routines. Evidence is mainly the title, changed-file list, and diff excerpt; there was no PR body or review-comment text in the slice.

title: Match __OSUnhandledException
author: r-burns
summary: Matched Dolphin OS __OSUnhandledException in C by removing the MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementation and its separate data assembly file, folding report strings and exception switch behavior into src/dolphin/os/OSError.c. The PR also moved the __DIRegs hardware register declaration from OSInit.c into OSInit.h so OSError.c could reference it, and removed asm/dolphin/os/OSError_data.s from obj_files.mk.

title: Match OSInit
author: r-burns
summary: Matched Dolphin OS initialization code by replacing a large inline-assembly/NOT_IMPLEMENTED OSInit implementation with C, moving OS_data.s data and sbss into OSInit.c, and cleaning related declarations in OSInit.h and db.{c,h}. The PR body says it was based on Prime decomp and still needed cleanup, and review feedback confirms some stylistic and type choices remained under discussion.

title: Support `-requireprotos`, pass 1
author: ribbanya
summary: First pass toward building with CodeWarrior's `-requireprotos` option. The PR mostly added missing function prototypes to headers across Dolphin AI/AR, sysdolphin baselib, and several fighter character modules, especially Master Hand, CLink, Crazy Hand, and Game & Watch. It also adjusted a few function declarations/definitions to use explicit `void` parameter lists, exposed `__ARHandler` by removing `static`, added per-C-file headers for newly prototyped functions, and normalized some `struct _HSD_GObj*` prototypes to `HSD_GObj*` after including the proper gobj header.

title: Improve `Makefile` command line flexibility
author: ribbanya
summary: Updated the root Makefile to make several compiler behaviors configurable from the command line. The PR added `REQUIRE_PROTOS` and `MSG_STYLE` variables, changed `-msgstyle gcc` to use `$(MSG_STYLE)`, conditionally adds `-requireprotos`, and adds `-nofail` when `MAX_ERRORS` is `0`. This improves build/debug ergonomics without touching decompiled source.

title: Match dolphin ar (except __ARChecksize)
author: r-burns
summary: Matched most of Dolphin's ARAM AR module in C, leaving __ARChecksize in assembly. The PR moved ARRegisterDMACallback, ARGetDMAStatus, ARStartDMA, ARAlloc, ARFree, ARCheckInit, ARInit, ARGetBaseAddress, ARGetSize, and __ARHandler from asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s into new src/dolphin/ar/ar.c, added ar.c.o to obj_files.mk before the remaining ar.s.o, and corrected ARRegisterDMACallback's header prototype to return the previous Event callback.

title: Fix `strict-prototypes` warnings
author: ribbanya
summary: Cleaned up the remaining clang strict-prototypes warnings caused by C declarations and definitions with empty parameter lists, while also tightening several callback and helper signatures across Dolphin OS/AR/GX, Melee fighter/stage/player/lb headers, and sysdolphin baselib animation code. The PR mostly converted `foo()` declarations to `foo(void)` for no-argument functions, added real parameter types or dummy parameter names where callbacks were intentionally unused, introduced shared baselib callback typing via `HSD_ObjUpdateFunc` and `HSD_ObjData`, and moved `OSException` into a header so exception handlers use one typed signature. The PR body notes that a few TExp warnings remained.

title: Match OSContext, OSInterrupt
author: r-burns
summary: Matched Dolphin OSContext and OSInterrupt work by converting large raw asm/data fragments into typed C/inline-asm sources, moving formerly separate data objects into C, and tightening shared headers for interrupt masks, context layout, hardware-register externs, and volatile interrupt state.

title: Fix `clang` errors
author: ribbanya
summary: Large portability/build-cleanup PR that fixed all clang errors, not warnings, across Runtime, Dolphin OS/GX/base/AI, sysdolphin baselib, fighter, item, stage, and map collision code. The dominant tactic was to isolate CodeWarrior/MWCC-only inline assembly and matching-specific constructs behind `MWERKS_GEKKO`, `__MWERKS__`, or `MUST_MATCH`, then provide clang-parseable `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks with corrected prototypes and headers. It also cleaned up several C type/prototype issues, flag macro naming, pointer-assignment expressions, and a baselib circular include via a small forward declaration header.

title: Match dolphin PadClamp, axfx
author: r-burns
summary: Matched two small Dolphin SDK areas by replacing assembly with C: PADClamp in the pad subsystem and AXFX allocation hook helpers in axfx. The PR removed asm/dolphin/pad/Padclamp.s and asm/dolphin/axfx/axfx.s from the build, added src/dolphin/axfx/axfx.c, extended src/dolphin/pad/PadClamp.c with PADClamp, and moved the __OSCurrHeap declaration into OSAlloc.h so AXFX code could use the OS allocator interface cleanly.

title: Match dolphin ARQ
author: r-burns
summary: Replaced the hand-written assembly implementation of Dolphin ARQ with a matching C implementation and public header. The PR removed asm/dolphin/ar/arq.s, added src/dolphin/ar/arq.c and src/dolphin/ar/arq.h, and updated obj_files.mk to build arq.c.o instead of arq.s.o. The implementation models ARQ high/low request queues, pending requests, callbacks, chunked low-priority DMA transfers, DMA interrupt servicing, initialization, posting requests under disabled interrupts, and ARQGetChunkSize. The PR body says it was based on Prime decomp. There were no review comments; one collaborator review approved it.

title: Fix `implicit-function-declaration` warnings, pass 14
author: ribbanya
summary: Broad warning-cleanup pass aimed at eliminating remaining `-Wimplicit-function-declaration` diagnostics, especially across sysdolphin baselib, Dolphin GX/MTX/OS headers, and a few fighter files. The PR promoted many local or missing declarations into headers, corrected include paths/order, moved GX prototypes to more appropriate public headers, added function-pointer typedef usage such as `Event`, and added MWERKS/non-MWERKS guards or stubs around inline assembly. Review feedback emphasized keeping aggregate initializers minimal by relying on C zero-initialization, avoiding unnecessary names for unused parameters, preserving uncertain symbol names unless backed by maps, and retaining some explicit parentheses where they silence clang warnings.

title: Fix `implicit-function-declaration` warnings, pass 13
author: ribbanya
summary: Pass 13 of the implicit-function-declaration cleanup added or moved declarations across many melee, sysdolphin, MSL, MetroTRK, Runtime, and dolphin headers/sources, while also renaming many assembly call targets from anonymous func_80368xxx CObj accessors to HSD_CObjGetInterest, HSD_CObjGetEyePosition, and HSD_CObjGetUpVector. The PR body notes that after this pass, only sysdolphin remained. Review discussion centered on an mpcoll.c preprocessor workaround, where an #else was changed to #elif false because the alternate C implementation had a bad signature; reviewers accepted leaving it until someone matches/fixes it.

title: Fix `implicit-function-declaration` warnings, pass 12
author: ribbanya
summary: Twelfth pass eliminating implicit-function-declaration warnings, with the PR body stating that `gr` and `it` were done. The change mainly tightened prototypes and includes across ground/stage and item code, converted many stage callback stubs from K&R/empty parameter lists or `s32` return placeholders to explicit typed signatures, added missing public declarations, and adjusted compile/editor warning handling. Review discussion focused on preserving readability and matching intent while satisfying clang/GCC constraints.

title: Match dolphin dsp_task

title: Implement `stdbool.h`
author: ribbanya
summary: Added an MSL `stdbool.h` shim and migrated the codebase from project-local `BOOL`/`TRUE`/`FALSE` in `Runtime/platform.h` to standard-style `bool`/`true`/`false`. title: Fix context syntax
author: ribbanya
systems: Runtime;MSL;MetroTRK;Dolphin OS init;Dolphin GX;Dolphin AI;fighter headers;m2ctx
summary: Cherry-picked from #670, this PR cleaned up C/header syntax so the codebase remained usable for context generation while preserving MWERKS-specific behavior where needed. The main pattern was to hide compiler-only constructs such as __declspec(section ".init"), address-qualified declarations, and unknown-size arrays behind cross-tool macros in Runtime/platform.h, then apply those macros across startup/runtime/TRK headers. It also moved FILE/__file_modes structure definitions into stdio.h, trimmed duplicate or misplaced declarations from wchar_io and GX unknown headers, added small GX public headers/prototypes, and standardized fighter action enums to start character-specific IDs at ASID_MAX instead of redefining AS_FTCOMMON = 340 in each character header.

summary: Labeled a broad set of previously anonymous Dolphin SDK assembly symbols across OdemuExi2/DebuggerDriver, HIO, MCC, VI, and related call sites. The PR mostly replaced address-based func_/lbl_ names with SDK-style function and data names, propagated those names through branch/call references, and adjusted a few data declarations and alignment boundaries so newly named symbols describe the intended storage units. There was no PR body or review discussion; the only recorded review was an approval.

title: Label dolphin MCC, AXFX
author: r-burns
summary: Large assembly-labeling PR that moved a previously ambiguous Dolphin audio block out of asm/dolphin/ax/ax_unk.s into named asm/dolphin/axfx files, labeled AXFX chorus/delay/reverb routines, and applied GameCube debug-ELF-derived MCC/FIO/HIO and related baselib symbol names. The PR mattered because it replaced thousands of func_/lbl_ placeholders and an explicitly uncertain ax_unk grouping with library-recognizable modules and entry points, improving future decompilation targeting and callgraph readability.

title: Create auto-publishing documentation using Doxygen

title: Use AT_ADDRESS macro for fixed-address declarations
author: r-burns
summary: Introduced a portable AT_ADDRESS(x) macro in include/dolphin/types.h and converted fixed-address declarations across Dolphin OS, DSP, GX, CARD, DVD, and serial/time code from MWCC-only address syntax such as ': 0xCC005000' to declarations using AT_ADDRESS. The PR body states the purpose directly: the macro expands differently for PERMUTER, __MWERKS__, and other compilers so fixed-address declarations remain usable with decomp-permuter and non-MWCC compilers.

title: Migrate `os` to C
author: ribbanya
summary: Large experimental migration of Dolphin `os` translation units from standalone `.s` assembly into C files, often using C with inline assembly plus separate `_data.s` files for remaining data sections. The stated goal was to make even very large TUs easier to work on incrementally: move `.text` into C first, create headers and provisional types, identify data, later migrate data and match functions. The PR removed full assembly sources such as `asm/dolphin/os/OSThread.s`, `OSReset.s`, `OSResetSW.s`, and `OSMemory.s`, added or expanded corresponding `src/dolphin/os/*.c` files, split data into files like `OSCache_data.s`, `OSContext_data.s`, `OSInterrupt_data.s`, and updated headers/build lists. Review discussion noted that much SDK OS code is handwritten assembly and therefore may be low-value or busy work for new contributors, while the author argued that placeholder `unk_t` signatures are useful because compiler errors force real signatures when symbols are used elsewhere.

author: r-burns
summary: Replaced many obvious hardcoded pi-related constants with the standard M_PI expression form across fighter, stage, and vector-library code. The PR removed local PI, PI64, and HALF_PI64-style defines where they were clearly equivalent, switched angle normalization and facing-direction logic to M_PI, 2 * M_PI, and M_PI / 2, and included math.h where needed. The author explicitly limited the pass to no-brainer cases and left float-typed degree-to-radian constants and other bit-sensitive cases for future discussion.

summary: Converted most of Young Link's ftclink assembly into C, removing 529 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.s and adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftclink.c plus a new ftclink.h. title: Add the modified calcprogress that is closer on r40
author: PsiLupan
summary: Updated the progress-calculation tool to report results closer to the r40 reference by refining which map-file sections and symbols count toward decompiled code/data size. The change adjusted data-section recognition, removed the generic "file" pseudo-section from DATA_SECTIONS, added _extab/_exidx handling, and added stateful object/symbol tracking so immediately preceding symbols can be subtracted when followed by an "entry of" line.

title: Add Cam's match of fmod

summary: Replaced an inline PowerPC assembly implementation of the local `fmod` helper in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c` with a short matching C implementation attributed by the PR title to Cam. It also renamed the exported dispatcher from func_80005B64 to Command_80005B64 at known asm callsites, expanded lbcommand.h prototypes, and refined CommandInfo field naming from unk_x10 to event_return.

title: Fix write error and handle other mr cases
author: PsiLupan
summary: Fixed a small but important bug in tools/postprocess.py: after reading the instruction at mr_pos, the script now seeks back to mr_pos before writing, preventing the write from landing at the post-read file offset. The postprocess pattern matcher was also broadened to recognize several valid `mr r3, r28/r29/r30/r31` encodings after an `lwz r0, N(r1)` instruction, instead of only one hard-coded `mr` case.

title: Fix postprocess assert caused by properly ordered code

summary: Switched the build to use the Metrowerks 1.3.2 linker while keeping the compiler at MWCC 1.0. The stated motivation was link-time speed: 1.3.2 was faster than the previous linker and less difficult to adopt than 2.7. title: Elf2dol wii changes
author: RevoSucks
summary: Updated the repo build pipeline and tools/elf2dol.c so elf2dol receives a target platform string and can account for Wii-style duplicated PDHR size handling while preserving the default GameCube build path. The Makefile now defines TARGET_COL as gamecube and passes it to ELF2DOL. elf2dol now tracks sdata and sbss sizes in two-element arrays and, when platform is "wii", also captures the sizes at sdata_pdhr + 2 and sbss_pdhr + 2 before adding all tracked sizes into the DOL BSS size.

title: Switch lcf to use template and refactor build system to use template.
author: RevoSucks
summary: Refactored the early Melee build/link pipeline so object link order moved out of the linker script into a new obj_files.mk, while ldscript.lcf switched from a long hand-maintained section/object listing to a template-style LCF with grouped aligned sections. title: Preprocess lcf and start purging lcf pointers
author: RevoSucks
summary: Made the linker command file preprocessable and began replacing hard-coded LCF section addresses with derived segment symbols/macros. The linker script now defines BEGIN_SEG/END_SEG macros, chains most section starts from prior segment ends, adds temporary hacks for sdata/sdata2 positioning, and computes _SDA_BASE_/_SDA2_BASE_ from linker-derived positions instead of fixed literal addresses. A matching-support symbol _SDA_BASE_START was added in asm/sdata2.s, and newline-only cleanup occurred in include/global.h and asm/bss.s.

title: setup postprocess.py
author: RevoSucks
summary: Added a Python ELF postprocessor and wired it into the C object build rule. The tool supports three CodeWarrior-oriented fixes: ctor/dtor section alignment repair, mangled-symbol character substitution reversal, and old-stack prologue/epilogue scheduling fixups. The Makefile now invokes python3 tools/postprocess.py after each C compilation with -fprologue-fixup=old_stack, and adds -nodefaults to CFLAGS.
